raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Assembly Bill 1911 aims to create a new tax credit program to preserve at least 25,000 units of affordable housing at risk of conversion to market-rate rents.",
    "An estimated 13.2 million adults living in rental housing are behind on rent payments due to the COVID-19 pandemic.",
    "Renters of color are facing the worst hardships related to housing during the COVID-19 pandemic.",
    "Over 31,000 subsidized affordable rental units in California are at risk of converting to market-rate units as state and federal subsidies expire over the next ten years.",
    "Between 1997 and 2019, California lost 15,004 affordable rental homes as subsidies and affordability restrictions expired.",
    "AB 1911 would incentivize owners of affordable housing to sell to organizations that commit to maintaining long-term affordability.",
    "The legislation would enable California to preserve roughly 25,000 affordable units over the next five years at a cost of approximately $20,000 per unit.",
    "The Affordable Housing Preservation Tax Credit is described as a tool that could significantly slow the loss of existing affordable housing in California."
  ]
}
```
